title: AI/AGI Intern
description:
Overview

We are seeking an AI/AGI Intern to join our team at Flo Labs R&D, where you'll play a key role in advancing artificial intelligence technologies for our humanoid robot. This position offers an unparalleled opportunity to contribute to pioneering AI and AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) research, helping shape the future of intelligent robotics and machine learning systems.

As an AI/AGI Intern, you will develop and optimize AI algorithms, conduct data analysis, and train machine learning models for real-world robotic applications. You will collaborate closely with robotics engineers, data scientists, and AI researchers, ensuring seamless integration of AI systems into autonomous robotic platforms.

Additionally, you will engage in continuous research and evaluation of emerging AI tools to enhance productivity, staying abreast of the latest technological advancements. This proactive approach ensures our team remains at the forefront of innovation, integrating cutting-edge solutions into our workflows. This internship is ideal for individuals passionate about AI-driven innovation, robotics, and scientific discovery.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and optimize AI and AGI algorithms for robotic decision-making, perception, and adaptability.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate AI systems into robotic platforms and real-time applications.

Conduct data analysis and machine learning model training, enhancing the robot’s ability to learn from its environment.

Explore deep learning architectures, including neural networks, reinforcement learning, and generative models.

Evaluate AI tools and frameworks, ensuring seamless integration with robot control and sensory systems.

Engage in academic research, co-authoring scientific papers and contributing to AI publications.

Document findings and collaborate with peers to drive AI-driven project development.

Participate in team meetings, brainstorming sessions, and workshops, contributing innovative ideas and problem-solving approaches.

Qualifications and Skills

Understanding of AI, machine learning, and neural networks, including deep learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch).

Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C++, or Java for AI model development.

Experience with data analysis, model training, and performance evaluation.

Familiarity with robotics platforms (e.g., ROS) and computer vision tools is a plus.

Problem-solving abilities and critical thinking skills for AI-driven challenges.

Ability to work effectively in a team environment, collaborating with researchers and engineers.

Interest in AI-powered robotics, autonomous systems, and AGI development.

Why Join Us?

Stay at the Forefront of Innovation: Explore the latest advancements in AI, AGI, and robotics, contributing to scientific publications and research papers.

Fully Remote & Flexible Schedule: This internship is entirely remote, allowing you to contribute from anywhere. You can choose to work full-time (up to 40 hours per week) or part-time (a minimum of 8-10 hours per week) based on your availability.

On-site Internship (Optional): If interested, we can offer onsite internships at two locations: Austin, Texas, and Lemnos, Greece.

Work on Cutting-Edge AI Projects: Contribute to AI-driven humanoid robotics, advancing machine learning, AGI algorithms, and real-world applications.

Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Work alongside AI researchers, data scientists, and robotics engineers, gaining hands-on experience in applied AI and robotics.

Hands-On Machine Learning & AI Development: Build practical skills in algorithm optimization, neural networks, and autonomous decision-making.

title: Animal Science and Veterinary Research Intern
description:
Overview

Are you passionate about animal care and excited by the potential of technology to improve animal wellness? Join our innovative team at Flo Labs R&D as an Animal Science and Veterinary Research Intern, where you'll contribute to cutting-edge projects that combine veterinary research, animal behavior studies, and AI-driven technology to enhance animal health and enrichment.

As an Animal Science and Veterinary Research Intern, you will work on AI-powered tools, wearable technology, and cognition studies, contributing to breakthroughs in animal wellness while collaborating with engineers, data scientists, and veterinarians.

Additionally, you will engage in continuous research and evaluation of emerging AI tools to enhance productivity, staying abreast of the latest technological advancements. This proactive approach ensures our team remains at the forefront of innovation, integrating cutting-edge solutions into our workflows.

What You’ll Work On:

Animal Communication Research (Vocalization Project):

Collaborate with AI teams developing tools to interpret and translate animal vocalizations, enhancing human-animal communication.

Analyze vocalization patterns, providing insights into animal behavior and emotional states.

Ensure that documentation is user-friendly, accurate, and up-to-date.

Wearables for Animal Wellness:

Work on advanced pet wearables equipped with health sensors for activity levels, heart rate, and sleep tracking.

Use data analysis to assess animal wellness, informing personalized care recommendations.

EEG Studies in Animal Cognition:

Participate in EEG-based research to study brain activity in cats and dogs, exploring animal cognition and emotional responses.

Investigate how animals respond to stimuli and communication attempts, contributing to cognitive breakthroughs.

Robotic Companions for Animal Enrichment:

Support the development of RoboDog and RoboCat, AI-driven robotic companions designed for pet enrichment and mental stimulation.

Contribute to research on robotic interaction, ensuring positive outcomes for animal welfare.

Cross-Disciplinary Pet Projects:

Collaborate with technology and robotics teams to ensure the seamless integration of veterinary insights into projects like CAIPO, an AI-driven health monitoring system.

Provide scientific expertise to inform the design and application of animal care technologies.

Qualifications and Skills

Pre-vet, veterinary medicine, or veterinary research background.

Focus on animal behavior, health, or wellness research.

Interest in applied animal research and pet care innovations.

Specialization in animal cognition, communication, and behavior analysis.

Familiarity with veterinary tools, diagnostic practices, or research methodologies.

Enthusiastic about advancing animal care through technology and interdisciplinary research.

Ability to work effectively across disciplines, integrating scientific knowledge with technological solutions.

Why Join Us?

Stay at the Forefront of Innovation: Explore the latest advancements in animal communication, health monitoring, and robotic enrichment technologies.

Fully Remote & Flexible Schedule: This internship is entirely remote, allowing you to contribute from anywhere. You can choose to work full-time (up to 40 hours per week) or part-time (a minimum of 8-10 hours per week) based on your availability.

On-site Internship (Optional): If interested, we can offer onsite internships at two locations: Austin, Texas, and Lemnos, Greece.

Shape the Future of Animal Care: Be part of pioneering research that will transform animal wellness technologies and pet care innovations.

Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Work alongside veterinarians, AI developers, and robotics engineers, gaining hands-on experience in animal-focused tech projects.

Hands-On Research Experience: Gain practical skills in veterinary research, animal cognition studies, and wearable technology testing.

title: Biomedical Engineering Intern
description:
Overview

We are seeking a Biomedical Engineering Intern to join our team at Flo Labs R&D, contributing to the development and refinement of advanced EEG sensor technology for our next-generation EEG headband. This role offers a unique opportunity to work on signal acquisition, processing, and hardware design, ensuring the highest accuracy and efficiency in brainwave data collection. As an intern, you will focus on enhancing signal processing techniques, reducing noise, and optimizing electrode design to improve overall sensor performance.

In addition to sensor development, you will collaborate closely with electrical engineers, software developers, and AI researchers to integrate the EEG sensors into a seamless, high-performance wearable device. You will conduct experiments and validation studies to assess sensor accuracy, reliability, and ergonomics, ensuring that our EEG headband meets both scientific and user-experience standards. This role provides valuable hands-on experience in biomedical signal processing, wearable device design, and neurotechnology research.

As part of our commitment to staying at the forefront of innovation, you will engage in continuous research and evaluation of emerging AI tools to enhance productivity, staying abreast of the latest technological advancements. This proactive approach ensures our team remains at the forefront of innovation, integrating cutting-edge solutions into our workflows.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and refine EEG sensor technology, including electrode design and signal acquisition.

Focus on signal processing techniques to enhance data quality, reduce noise, and improve real-time accuracy.

Collaborate with electrical and software engineering teams to ensure seamless sensor integration into wearable devices.

Conduct experiments and validation studies to test and optimize EEG sensor accuracy and reliability.

Assist in the design and development of ergonomic, wearable hardware for user-friendly application.

Stay informed on emerging AI and biomedical engineering tools, integrating innovative solutions into our research and development.

Participate in team meetings, brainstorming sessions, and workshops, contributing innovative ideas and problem-solving approaches.

Qualifications and Skills

Strong foundation in biomedical engineering principles and signal processing.

Experience in scientific research and data analysis, particularly in EEG technology and bio-signal acquisition.

Familiarity with biomedical sensors, electrode design, and wearable health technology is a plus

Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to work in a team-oriented environment.

Interest in AI applications in biomedical research and neurotechnology innovation.

Why Join Us?

Fully Remote & Flexible Schedule: This internship is entirely remote, allowing you to contribute from anywhere. You can choose to work full-time (up to 40 hours per week) or part-time (a minimum of 8-10 hours per week) based on your availability.

On-site Internship (Optional): If interested, we can offer onsite internships at two locations: Austin, Texas, and Lemnos, Greece.

Work on Cutting-Edge Neurotechnology: Contribute to the development of advanced EEG wearables that push the boundaries of brainwave monitoring.

Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Work alongside biomedical engineers, neuroscientists, AI researchers, and electrical engineers to bring innovative ideas to life.

Hands-On Research & Development: Gain experience in sensor technology, neurotechnology validation, and biomedical signal processing.

Real-World Impact: Help create next-generation wearable devices that improve brain health, neurofeedback, and cognitive enhancement.

title: Computer Vision Engineering Intern
description:
Overview

We are seeking a Computer Vision Engineering Intern to join our team at Flo Labs R&D, contributing to the development of the visual processing capabilities of our AGI humanoid robot. This role provides an exciting opportunity to work on advanced computer vision algorithms that enable robots to perceive, understand, and interact with their surroundings in real-time.

Most recently, we’ve launched a new project focused on the development of our own humanoid robot - a major milestone in our mission to push the boundaries of intelligent systems and human-robot interaction. Interns who join our program may have the opportunity to contribute directly to this groundbreaking initiative.

As a Computer Vision Engineering Intern, you will work on object detection, scene recognition, and spatial navigation algorithms, ensuring our humanoid robot can accurately interpret its environment and make intelligent decisions. You will collaborate closely with AI researchers, roboticists, and software engineers to integrate vision systems into a fully functional robotic intelligence framework.

Additionally, you will engage in continuous research and evaluation of emerging AI tools to enhance productivity, staying abreast of the latest technological advancements. This proactive approach ensures our team remains at the forefront of innovation, integrating cutting-edge solutions into our workflows. If you're passionate about robotics, computer vision, and AI-driven perception, this internship offers a unique hands-on experience in a rapidly evolving field.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and optimize computer vision algorithms for object detection, facial recognition, and scene understanding.

Integrate vision systems with other robotic components, ensuring seamless interaction with motion planning, sensor fusion, and AI decision-making systems.

Conduct experiments and analyze data to improve vision system accuracy, speed, and robustness.

Research deep learning models (CNNs, Transformers, Optical Flow, SLAM) to enhance robotic perception capabilities.

Implement real-time image processing techniques for gesture recognition, gaze tracking, and environmental mapping.

Collaborate with AI researchers, roboticists, and software engineers to integrate vision-based intelligence into our humanoid robot.

Participate in team meetings, brainstorming sessions, and workshops, contributing innovative ideas and problem-solving approaches.

Qualifications and Skills

Familiarity with computer vision libraries (e.g., OpenCV, TensorFlow, PyTorch, ROS, Open3D).

Strong programming skills in Python or C++, with experience in deep learning frameworks.

Knowledge of 3D vision, stereo imaging, SLAM, or real-time image processing is a plus.

Creative problem-solving abilities and a strong analytical mindset.

Ability to work effectively in a team environment, collaborating on interdisciplinary projects.

Passion for robotics, AGI, and AI-driven perception systems.

Why Join Us?

Stay at the Forefront of Innovation: Research and implement the latest advancements in AI-driven perception, SLAM, and neural networks.

Fully Remote & Flexible Schedule: This internship is entirely remote, allowing you to contribute from anywhere. You can choose to work full-time (up to 40 hours per week) or part-time (a minimum of 8-10 hours per week) based on your availability.

On-site Internship (Optional): If interested, we can offer onsite internships at two locations: Austin, Texas, and Lemnos, Greece.

Work on Cutting-Edge Robotics: Contribute to the development of AGI-powered humanoid robots with advanced vision systems.

Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Work alongside computer vision engineers, AI researchers, roboticists, and software developers.

Hands-On Experience with AI & Robotics: Gain real-world exposure to deep learning, real-time image processing, and robotic vision.

title: Embedded Systems Intern
description:
Overview

We are seeking an Embedded Systems Intern to join our team at Flo Labs R&D, playing a crucial role in the development of firmware and microcontroller programming for our humanoid robot. This position provides a hands-on opportunity to work on low-level programming, real-time embedded systems, and hardware-software integration, ensuring the seamless operation and functionality of our robotic platform.

As an Embedded Systems Intern, you will be responsible for developing and testing firmware, interfacing microcontrollers with sensors, actuators, and communication protocols, and collaborating with hardware and software engineers to optimize system performance. You will gain exposure to real-time operating systems (RTOS), embedded AI, and edge computing, working on power-efficient and high-performance firmware solutions.

Additionally, you will engage in continuous research and evaluation of emerging AI tools to enhance productivity, staying abreast of the latest technological advancements. This proactive approach ensures our team remains at the forefront of innovation, integrating cutting-edge solutions into our workflows. If you're passionate about embedded programming, robotics, and AI-integrated firmware, this internship provides an exciting learning opportunity in a rapidly evolving field.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and test firmware for embedded systems in our humanoid robot.

Program microcontrollers (ARM Cortex, ESP32, STM32, or AVR) and interface them with sensors, actuators, and peripherals.

Work on real-time embedded systems, ensuring low-latency and power-efficient performance.

Conduct debugging, troubleshooting, and system validation using oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, and firmware debugging tools.

Collaborate with hardware, software, and robotics teams to ensure seamless system integration.

Document firmware development processes, testing results, and optimizations for future reference and knowledge sharing.

Participate in team meetings, brainstorming sessions, and workshops, contributing innovative ideas and problem-solving approaches.

Qualifications and Skills

Experience with microcontrollers and embedded programming (ARM Cortex, ESP32, STM32, AVR, or similar).

Familiarity with C or C++ for low-level programming and firmware development.

Strong understanding of hardware interfaces (I2C, SPI, UART, CAN, PWM, ADC/DAC) and embedded communication protocols.

Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and creative problem-solving abilities.

Ability to work effectively in a team environment, collaborating with engineers across multiple disciplines.

Why Join Us?

Stay at the Forefront of Innovation: Work with real-time systems, power-efficient firmware, and AI-driven embedded applications.

Fully Remote & Flexible Schedule: This internship is entirely remote, allowing you to contribute from anywhere. You can choose to work full-time (up to 40 hours per week) or part-time (a minimum of 8-10 hours per week) based on your availability.

On-site Internship (Optional): If interested, we can offer onsite internships at two locations: Austin, Texas, and Lemnos, Greece.

Work on Cutting-Edge Robotics: Be part of a team developing AI-driven humanoid robots with advanced embedded systems.

Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Work alongside hardware engineers, roboticists, AI researchers, and software developers.

Hands-On Embedded Development: Gain real-world experience in firmware development, microcontroller programming, and system optimization.

title: Space Science Intern
description:
About the Role
Are you passionate about space, planetary defense, or the search for life beyond Earth? Join Flo Labs R&D’s internship program and work on bold, hands-on projects like asteroid deflection, deep-space robotics, and extraterrestrial exploration—regardless of your academic background. If you're curious, creative, and eager to learn, this is your launchpad.

What You'll Do

Participate in designing and testing robotic systems for planetary defense and asteroid interception.

Help simulate and model spacecraft and satellite missions using real data.

Research technologies for robotic space mining, lunar bases, and deep-space travel.

Contribute to AI- and robotics-based search-for-life initiatives (biosignature detection, signal decoding).

Assist with mission planning tools, orbital simulations, or robotic deployment strategies.

Collaborate in interdisciplinary teams using engineering, programming, astronomy, and biology perspectives.

What We’re Looking For

Passionate about space science, space exploration, or interplanetary defense.

Willing to learn and dive into unfamiliar tools and technologies.

Basic familiarity with programming, physics, or engineering concepts is helpful—but not required.

Curious, persistent, and able to work independently and collaboratively.

Learning First
Before working on projects, all selected interns complete our custom Space Science Masterclass—an online learning experience with curated free content from YouTube, NASA, ESA, and space science thought leaders. You’ll explore topics like:

Planetary motion & celestial mechanics

Astrobiology & biosignature detection

Robotic systems for space missions

Satellite design & orbital dynamics

AI & data science in astronomy

Why This Internship Matters
Our long-term mission is to explore the universe with AGI-enabled humanoids, defend Earth from hazardous asteroids, and pursue answers to humanity’s deepest questions—Who created us? Are we alone? Why are we here?

This internship is a way to gain real experience contributing to those efforts—through simulation, research, prototyping, and exploration.

Join Us

Collaborate with global teams in a remote-first environment

Work on real-world space science projects

Build skills across robotics, AI, astronomy, and planetary systems

Earn eligibility for our Earn & Learn program after successful completion

—

If this inspires you, visit our site to explore our current projects and apply.

Passion for AI, robotics, and sensor technologies applied to sports performance and injury prevention.

Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail, particularly in data interpretation and project execution.

Self-driven and enthusiastic, with a strong desire to learn and contribute to innovative projects.

Experience: Exposure to machine learning or AI algorithms for data-driven sports performance analysis is a plus.

title: Technical Writing Intern
